<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Settings interface redesigned with sidebar navigation, section-driven content areas, compact responsive layout, and consolidated actions for simpler configuration and index management.

* **Documentation**
  * Changelog updated with entries describing the Settings UX redesign and embedding/index metadata behavior.

* **Tests**
  * Expanded frontend and backend test suites covering settings navigation, embedding/indexing behaviors, cache and API edge cases, and indexing progress/error scenarios.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Walkthrough

Redesigned the SettingsModal to a sidebar + content-pane layout, added/updated extensive frontend tests, and expanded backend tests for indexing, embedding, cache, and API edge cases; also documented FAISS embedding-dimension checks and index metadata persistence in the changelog.

## Changes

|Cohort / File(s)|Summary|
|---|---|
|**Changelog** <br> `AGENTS.md`|Added entries for "Settings UX Redesign" and "FAISS Dimension Safety & Test Fixes" describing UI layout changes, embedding-dimension error behavior, and index metadata persistence.|
|**Settings Modal UI** <br> `frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`|Large refactor: replace previous modal chrome with left navigation (`settingsSections`) + right content pane, introduce `activeSection`, consolidate sections (Indexed Folders, AI Provider, Embedding Provider, Local Models, Data Management), simplify per-folder controls, and change top/bottom action placement and styling.|
|**Frontend tests (Settings)** <br> `frontend/src/test/SettingsModal.test.jsx`|Expanded and adjusted tests to exercise new section-driven navigation, embedding/provider flows, local-model UI, indexing actions, and data-management interactions; small test tweak to navigate to Data Management before clearing history.|
|**Backend tests — API & Indexing** <br> `backend/tests/test_api.py`, `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`|Added many API tests (embedding settings, folder browsing, cache endpoints, dimension-mismatch handling returning 409), indexing tests verifying embedding-client usage, save/load index metadata sidecar, progress callbacks, and error cases.|
|**Backend tests — Database Cache** <br> `backend/tests/test_database.py`|New tests for response cache: caching, hit counts, misses, and clearing behavior.|
|**Test Duplicates / Caveats** <br> `backend/tests/test_api.py`|Duplicate embedding settings test class appears twice in the diff (identical declarations) — may require deduplication. |

## Sequence Diagram(s)

mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Client as Client (Browser/UI)
  participant API as Server API
  participant FAISS as FAISS Index
  participant DB as Database / Index Storage

  Client->>API: search request with query + embedding vector
  API->>FAISS: check index embedding_dim vs provided vector
  alt dimension mismatch
    FAISS-->>API: mismatch detected
    API-->>Client: 409 Conflict + EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError (detail: embedding_dim)
  else dimensions match
    FAISS-->>API: search results
    API->>DB: (optionally) read index metadata (_meta.json)
    API-->>Client: search results

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~45 minutes

## Possibly related PRs

- BhurkeSiddhesh/Docu-AI-Search#9 — Related changes to backend indexing persistence and save/load index behavior (metadata sidecar and load return values).
- BhurkeSiddhesh/Docu-AI-Search#11 — Related changes to indexing tests and test scaffolding/mocks for save/load and embedding behavior.

## Poem

> 🐰  
> In a burrow of code I hopped with glee,  
> Sidebar sprouted where tabs used to be,  
> Indexes whisper their dim-safe song,  
> Tests hop along to make sure nothing's wrong,  
> I nibble a carrot and push the changelog free.

</details>

author:	coderabbitai
association:	contributor
edited:	false
status:	changes requested
--
**Actionable comments posted: 4**

> [!CAUTION]
> Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations.
> 
> 
> 
> <details>
> <summary>⚠️ Outside diff range comments (2)</summary><blockquote>
> 
> <details>
> <summary>frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx (2)</summary><blockquote>
> 
> `143-168`: _🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_
> 
> **Inconsistent API base URL usage.**
> 
> Several API calls use hardcoded URLs instead of the `API` constant defined at line 6. This violates the DRY principle and the coding guideline requiring axios calls to use the `API` constant. Affected locations: lines 145, 154, 163, and 273.
> 
> <details>
> <summary>♻️ Proposed fix</summary>
> 
> ```diff
>  const fetchIndexingStatus = async () => {
>      try {
> -        const response = await axios.get('http://localhost:8000/api/index/status');
> +        const response = await axios.get(`${API}/api/index/status`);
>          setIndexingStatus(response.data);
>      } catch (error) {
>          console.error('Failed to fetch status:', error);
>      }
>  };
> 
>  const fetchFolderHistory = async () => {
>      try {
> -        const response = await axios.get('http://localhost:8000/api/folders/history');
> +        const response = await axios.get(`${API}/api/folders/history`);
>          setFolderHistory(response.data || []);
>      } catch (error) {
>          console.error('Failed to fetch folder history:', error);
>      }
>  };
> 
>  const fetchCacheStats = async () => {
>      try {
> -        const response = await axios.get('http://localhost:8000/api/cache/stats');
> +        const response = await axios.get(`${API}/api/cache/stats`);
>          setCacheStats(response.data);
>      } catch (error) {
>          console.error('Failed to fetch cache stats:', error);
>      }
>  };
> ```
> 
> Also fix line 273:
> ```diff
>  const handleIndex = async () => {
>      await handleSave();
>      try {
> -        await axios.post('http://localhost:8000/api/index');
> +        await axios.post(`${API}/api/index`);
>      } catch (error) {
>          console.error('Failed to index:', error);
>      }
>  };
> ```
> </details>
> 
> As per coding guidelines: "API calls from frontend must use axios with `const API = 'http://localhost:8000'` base URL"
> 
> <details>
> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>
> 
> ```
> Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.
> 
> In `@frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx` around lines 143 - 168, Replace
> the hardcoded base URL in the three async functions fetchIndexingStatus,
> fetchFolderHistory, and fetchCacheStats to use the existing API constant (const
> API = 'http://localhost:8000') instead of 'http://localhost:8000'; update
> axios.get calls to axios.get(`${API}/api/index/status`),
> axios.get(`${API}/api/folders/history`), and axios.get(`${API}/api/cache/stats`)
> respectively (or create an axios instance with baseURL: API and use relative
> paths). Also update the other hardcoded axios call referenced in the review (the
> additional call in this component near the bottom) to use the same API constant
> so all frontend calls use the shared base URL.
> ```
> 
> </details>
> 
> ---
> 
> `170-197`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_
> 
> **Clarify backend behavior when API key is not modified: non-local providers already enforce re-entry; consider preserving existing key for local provider.**
> 
> When the frontend omits `api_key` (placeholder detected), the backend sets it to an empty string via `body.api_key or ""` at line 147. However, this only affects the **local provider**:
> 
> - **Non-local providers** (huggingface_api, commercial_api): Validation at lines 137–142 rejects missing `api_key` with HTTP 422, forcing the user to re-enter it. The key is not overwritten.
> - **Local provider**: No validation for `api_key`, so an omitted key silently overwrites any existing value with an empty string.
> 
> Since the local provider rarely stores keys, this is an edge case. However, for consistency and safety, consider preserving the existing `api_key` when it's not provided in the request instead of defaulting to an empty string.
> 
> <details>
> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>
> 
> ```
> Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.
> 
> In `@frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx` around lines 170 - 197, The
> backend currently defaults missing api_key to an empty string when handling POST
> /api/settings/embeddings (using body.api_key || ""), which causes the local
> provider to silently overwrite an existing key; change the update logic so that
> if the request does not include an api_key field you do not modify the stored
> key (i.e., only set or clear the stored value when body has an explicit api_key
> property), keep the existing value for the local provider when api_key is
> omitted, and retain existing validation for non-local providers
> (huggingface_api, commercial_api) that still require an explicit api_key.
> ```
> 
> </details>
> 
> </blockquote></details>
> 
> </blockquote></details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@AGENTS.md`:
- Around line 308-315: Add a single blank line immediately above the heading
"### 2026-03-12 (Settings UX Redesign)" in AGENTS.md to satisfy markdown lint
rule MD022; locate the heading text and insert one empty line before it so the
file has a blank line separating the previous paragraph/list from this heading.

In `@frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`:
- Around line 335-341: The Close button in SettingsModal.jsx (the JSX element
rendering <button onClick={onClose} ...><X .../></button>) and several other
buttons in this component are missing an explicit type, which can default to
type="submit" and cause unintended form submissions; update that Close button
and every other button in this component (the ones that call handlers like
onClose and the other action buttons referenced around lines noted in the
review) to include type="button" attribute to prevent form submission, leaving
behavior of any intentional submit buttons unchanged.
- Around line 488-499: The label/input pairs in the API provider mapping inside
SettingsModal.jsx are not connected for accessibility; update the JSX inside the
apiProviders.map block to give each input a unique id (e.g., derived from
provider.key or provider.id) and set the label's htmlFor to that same id so
screen readers associate the <label> with the corresponding input; keep using
the existing setConfig handler and provider.key as the value prop so only
attributes (id on the <input> and htmlFor on the <label>) are added/linked.
- Around line 321-326: The backdrop div with the existing onClick handler should
also handle keyboard close so keyboard-only users can dismiss the modal: add an
onKeyDown handler on the same element that checks for the 'Escape' key and calls
onClose(), and make the backdrop focusable by adding a tabIndex (e.g.,
tabIndex={-1} or 0) so it can receive keyboard events; ensure the handler only
closes when appropriate (e.target === e.currentTarget or similar) to avoid
closing when inner content is focused and do not interfere with other keys or
event propagation.

---

Outside diff comments:
In `@frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`:
- Around line 143-168: Replace the hardcoded base URL in the three async
functions fetchIndexingStatus, fetchFolderHistory, and fetchCacheStats to use
the existing API constant (const API = 'http://localhost:8000') instead of
'http://localhost:8000'; update axios.get calls to
axios.get(`${API}/api/index/status`), axios.get(`${API}/api/folders/history`),
and axios.get(`${API}/api/cache/stats`) respectively (or create an axios
instance with baseURL: API and use relative paths). Also update the other
hardcoded axios call referenced in the review (the additional call in this
component near the bottom) to use the same API constant so all frontend calls
use the shared base URL.
- Around line 170-197: The backend currently defaults missing api_key to an
empty string when handling POST /api/settings/embeddings (using body.api_key ||
""), which causes the local provider to silently overwrite an existing key;
change the update logic so that if the request does not include an api_key field
you do not modify the stored key (i.e., only set or clear the stored value when
body has an explicit api_key property), keep the existing value for the local
provider when api_key is omitted, and retain existing validation for non-local
providers (huggingface_api, commercial_api) that still require an explicit
api_key.
```

</details>

`1-48`: **LGTM!**

The imports, Toast component, and embedding configuration constants are well-structured. The `EMBEDDING_PROVIDER_TYPES` array correctly aligns with the backend's valid provider types, and `DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_CONFIG` matches the expected schema.

---

`311-317`: **LGTM! Well-structured navigation implementation.**

The `settingsSections` array and the sidebar navigation provide a clean, maintainable approach to the two-pane layout. The active state styling is clear, and the icons enhance discoverability. The grid layout (`md:grid-cols-[260px_1fr]`) provides good responsive behavior.



Also applies to: 344-366

</blockquote></details>
<details>
<summary>frontend/src/test/SettingsModal.test.jsx (2)</summary><blockquote>

`95-102`: **LGTM! Test correctly updated for new navigation flow.**

The test now navigates to the "Data Management" section before clicking "Clear Search History", which accurately reflects the redesigned UI where this action is scoped to a specific section. This maintains test coverage while adapting to the new layout.

---

`34-55`: **LGTM! Comprehensive mock setup.**

The mock configuration correctly includes `mockEmbeddingConfig` matching the backend's response format (with `api_key_set` boolean), and the `beforeEach` block properly mocks all required API endpoints including the new `/api/settings/embeddings`. This ensures tests accurately reflect the component's behavior.

</blockquote></details>

**Actionable comments posted: 7**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@backend/tests/test_api.py`:
- Around line 945-950: The test sets mock_search.side_effect to
EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError incorrectly using a single string; instead
instantiate EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError with the two integer parameters it
expects (query_dim and index_dim) so the exception is raised correctly and the
route can hit the 409 branch; update the mock assignment
(mock_search.side_effect = EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError(query_dim,
index_dim)) using the appropriate integers (e.g., 384 and 768) referencing the
EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError class and mock_search in this test.
- Around line 791-857: The tests leak embedding state because the real handler
caches config on request.app.state.embedding_config; wrap the test class to
snapshot and restore that state: in setUp (or before creating TestClient) save
current app.state.embedding_config (if exists) and ensure TestClient uses a
fresh/cleared embedding_config, and in tearDown restore the saved value (or
delete the temporary attribute) so methods like test_get_embedding_settings,
test_update_embedding_settings_local, test_update_embedding_settings_commercial,
test_update_embedding_settings_missing_api_key and
test_update_embedding_settings_invalid_provider do not share state across tests.

In `@backend/tests/test_database.py`:
- Around line 494-506: The test test_cache_hit_increments_count uses
cache_response, get_cached_response and get_cache_stats but only asserts
stats['total_hits'] > 0; change it to assert the exact expected total: since
cache_response inserts with hit_count = 1 and you call get_cached_response twice
(two increments), assert that get_cache_stats()['total_hits'] == 3 to ensure
reads increment the counter correctly.
- Around line 586-615: The test currently only checks the shape of
cleanup_test_data()'s return value; modify test_cleanup_test_data to verify the
inserted rows are actually removed by (1) capturing identifiers or locating the
inserted records immediately after database.add_file,
database.add_folder_to_history, and database.add_search_history (use return
values from those calls or helper lookups such as a
get_file_by_path/get_folder_history/get_search_history function), (2) call
database.cleanup_test_data(), and (3) assert those specific records are no
longer found (e.g. lookups return None/empty) and that counts returned by
cleanup_test_data() reflect the removed rows. Ensure you reference the same
symbols: test_cleanup_test_data, database.add_file,
database.add_folder_to_history, database.add_search_history, and
database.cleanup_test_data when adding the assertions.

In `@backend/tests/test_indexing.py`:
- Around line 1119-1133: The new test classes' setUp/tearDown create real worker
pools because they don't apply the synchronous executor patches used elsewhere,
causing non-deterministic create_index() runs; update these classes (the setUp
where temp files are created) to apply the same monkeypatch/patch for
concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor, concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor,
and concurrent.futures.as_completed that earlier tests use (or call the shared
helper/fixture) so create_index() runs synchronously and deterministically;
mirror the exact patching mechanism used by the earlier classes for both
occurrences mentioned (around the setUp at 1119-1133 and the other class at
1184-1193).
- Around line 956-985: Patch backend.indexing.get_embeddings in
test_create_index_with_embedding_client and set it to a MagicMock so the real
embedding path is not executed, then assert get_embeddings.assert_not_called()
after calling create_index; keep the existing mock_embedding_client and assert
mock_embedding_client.embed_documents.assert_called() to verify the provided
embedding_client was used instead of get_embeddings when invoking create_index.

In `@frontend/src/test/SettingsModal.test.jsx`:
- Around line 399-418: The test's final assertion is vacuous; update it to
verify the specific browse request was made and a visible UI outcome occurred:
after clicking the "Add Folder" button, assert axios.get was called with a URL
containing '/api/browse' (e.g.
expect(axios.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.stringContaining('/api/browse'),
expect.anything())), and assert a visible post-click outcome such as the error
text from the mocked rejection ('Failed to open dialog') appears (use waitFor(()
=> screen.getByText('Failed to open dialog')) ) or that the add-folder
input/modal did not open (e.g. expect(screen.queryByLabelText(/folder
path/i)).toBeNull()). Ensure these assertions replace or supplement the current
expect(axios.get).toHaveBeenCalled() check in the "handles browse folder error
gracefully" test.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>backend/**/*.py</summary>


**📄 CodeRabbit inference engine (.github/copilot-instructions.md)**

> `backend/**/*.py`: Backend code should be placed in `backend/` directory including `api.py`, `database.py`, `indexing.py`, `search.py`, `file_processing.py`, `llm_integration.py`, `model_manager.py`, `background.py`
> Python code should target Python 3.8+ compatibility while CI uses Python 3.10
> 
> `backend/**/*.py`: Redact sensitive search queries and PII in logs to prevent data leakage
> Implement lazy loading for heavy backend modules (langchain, llama-cpp, tkinter) to reduce cold-start time

Files:
- `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`
- `backend/tests/test_database.py`
- `backend/tests/test_api.py`

</details>
<details>
<summary>backend/tests/**/*.py</summary>


**📄 CodeRabbit inference engine (.github/copilot-instructions.md)**

> `backend/tests/**/*.py`: Backend tests should be placed in `backend/tests/` directory
> Mock LLM and network calls in backend tests
> Tests that manipulate DB should auto-use SQLite in `data/metadata.db` and clean via `database.cleanup_test_data()` on success
> 
> `backend/tests/**/*.py`: Backend tests MUST be placed in `backend/tests/` and follow pytest conventions
> Always mock LLM calls in tests to avoid API costs using `@patch` decorator

Files:
- `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`
- `backend/tests/test_database.py`
- `backend/tests/test_api.py`

</details>

**📄 CodeRabbit inference engine (AGENTS.md)**

> `**/*.py`: Backend code MUST be placed in `backend/` or `backend/tests/` directories. Exception: `api.py` is the entry point (in `backend/`). NO `.py` files in the project root
> All generated files (`.db`, `.faiss`, `.log`, `.json` results) MUST go in `data/` directory and code must reference `DATA_DIR` constant

<summary>📚 Learning: 2026-02-25T17:05:49.518Z</summary>

```

**Applied to files:**
- `backend/tests/test_api.py`

<!-- <requirement>
[requirement fix_settings_modal_accessibility_4/8]
In `frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`, fix three accessibility issues:

1. **Backdrop keyboard handler** (around Line 321-326): Add `onKeyDown` (checking for `'Escape'` key) and `tabIndex={-1}` to the backdrop `div`.
2. **Button types** (Lines 335, 351, 405, 411, 424, 435, 461, 474, 587, 594, 607, 613): Add `type="button"` to every button that lacks an explicit type.
3. **Label/input association** (around Line 488-499): In the `apiProviders.map` block, add `id={`api-key-${provider.id}`}` to each `<input>` and `htmlFor={`api-key-${provider.id}`}` to each `<label>`.



</requirement> -->

<!-- <requirement>
[requirement fix_test_api_embedding_state_isolation_5/8]
In `backend/tests/test_api.py`, add a `tearDown` method to `TestAPIEmbeddingSettings` that snapshots and restores `app.state.embedding_config` to prevent state leaking between tests:

```python
def setUp(self):
    self.client = TestClient(app)
    self._had_embedding_config = hasattr(app.state, 'embedding_config')
    self._original_embedding_config = getattr(app.state, 'embedding_config', None)

def tearDown(self):
    self.client.close()
    if self._had_embedding_config:
        app.state.embedding_config = self._original_embedding_config
    elif hasattr(app.state, 'embedding_config'):
        delattr(app.state, 'embedding_config')
```

Also fix the `EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError` instantiation around line 945-950 — change:
```python
mock_search.side_effect = EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError("Query dimension (384) != Index dimension (768)")
```
to:
```python
mock_search.side_effect = EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError(384, 768)
```



</requirement> -->

<!-- <requirement>
[requirement fix_test_database_assertions_6/8]
In `backend/tests/test_database.py`:

1. In `test_cache_hit_increments_count` (around line 494-506), replace the weak assertion:
   ```python
   self.assertGreater(stats['total_hits'], 0)
   ```
   with the precise assertion:
   ```python
   self.assertEqual(stats['total_entries'], 1)
   self.assertEqual(stats['total_hits'], 3)
   ```

2. In `test_cleanup_test_data` (around line 586-615), after calling `database.cleanup_test_data()`, add assertions that verify:
   - `counts['files'] >= 1`, `counts['folders'] >= 1`, `counts['search_history'] >= 1`
   - The inserted file/folder/query are actually gone (using available lookup helpers like `get_file_by_path`, `get_folder_history`, `get_search_history`)



</requirement> -->

<!-- <requirement>
[requirement fix_test_indexing_7/8]
In `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`:

1. In `test_create_index_with_embedding_client` (around line 956-985), add `@patch('backend.indexing.get_embeddings')` decorator and `mock_get_embeddings` parameter, then after calling `create_index`, assert:
   ```python
   mock_get_embeddings.assert_not_called()
   mock_embedding_client.embed_documents.assert_called()
   ```

2. In `TestSaveIndexWithMetadata.setUp` (around line 1119-1133) and `TestLoadIndexWithMetadata.setUp` (around line 1184-1193), apply the same synchronous executor patches used by earlier test classes:
   ```python
   self.pp_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor', side_effect=MockExecutor)
   self.tp_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor', side_effect=MockExecutor)
   self.ac_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.as_completed', side_effect=lambda fs: fs)
   self.pp_patcher.start(); self.tp_patcher.start(); self.ac_patcher.start()
   ```
   and stop them in `tearDown`.



</requirement> -->

await waitFor(() => expect(axios.get).toHaveBeenCalled())

await waitFor(() => {

grep -n "^def get_" backend/database.py | head -30
```

## Fix 1 — `AGENTS.md`: Add blank line before heading (MD022)

```diff
 > **CRITICAL: Add entry here after EVERY change with date, description, and files.**
 
+
 ### 2026-03-12 (Settings UX Redesign)
```

---

## Fix 2 — `frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`: Replace hardcoded URLs with `${API}` constant

Lines 145, 154, 163, and 273:

```diff
-    const response = await axios.get('http://localhost:8000/api/index/status');
+    const response = await axios.get(`${API}/api/index/status`);

-    const response = await axios.get('http://localhost:8000/api/folders/history');
+    const response = await axios.get(`${API}/api/folders/history`);

-    const response = await axios.get('http://localhost:8000/api/cache/stats');
+    const response = await axios.get(`${API}/api/cache/stats`);

-    await axios.post('http://localhost:8000/api/index');
+    await axios.post(`${API}/api/index`);
```

---

## Fix 3 — `frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`: Keyboard + focus accessibility for backdrop (Line ~321)

```diff
 <div
     className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/70 backdrop-blur-sm"
     onClick={(e) => {
         if (e.target === e.currentTarget) onClose();
     }}
+    onKeyDown={(e) => {
+        if (e.key === 'Escape' && e.target === e.currentTarget) onClose();
+    }}
+    role="button"
+    tabIndex={-1}
+    aria-label="Close settings modal"
 >
```

---

## Fix 4 — `frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`: Add `type="button"` to all buttons

Apply `type="button"` to every `<button>` in the component that lacks an explicit type. Confirmed affected lines: 31, 335, 351, 405, 411, 424, 435, 461, 474, 587, 594, 607, 613. Example pattern:

```diff
-<button
+<button
+    type="button"
     onClick={onClose}
     className="p-2 rounded-xl hover:bg-secondary transition-colors"
     aria-label="Close settings"
 >
```

Apply the same `type="button"` addition to every `<button>` at those lines.

---

## Fix 5 — `frontend/src/components/SettingsModal.jsx`: Associate labels with inputs (Line ~490)

```diff
 {apiProviders.map(provider => (
     <div key={provider.id} className="space-y-1">
-        <label className="text-sm font-medium">{provider.name}</label>
+        <label htmlFor={`api-key-${provider.id}`} className="text-sm font-medium">{provider.name}</label>
         <input
+            id={`api-key-${provider.id}`}
             type="password"
             value={config[provider.key] || ''}
             onChange={(e) => setConfig({ ...config, [provider.key]: e.target.value })}
             className="w-full px-3 py-2 text-sm rounded-lg border border-input bg-background"
             placeholder={provider.placeholder}
         />
     </div>
 ))}
```

---

## Fix 6 — `backend/tests/test_api.py`: Add state isolation to `TestAPIEmbeddingSettings` + fix `EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError` signature

```diff
 class TestAPIEmbeddingSettings(unittest.TestCase):
     """Test cases for embedding settings endpoints."""

     def setUp(self):
         """Set up test client before each test method."""
         self.client = TestClient(app)
+        self._had_embedding_config = hasattr(app.state, 'embedding_config')
+        self._original_embedding_config = getattr(app.state, 'embedding_config', None)
+
+    def tearDown(self):
+        self.client.close()
+        if self._had_embedding_config:
+            app.state.embedding_config = self._original_embedding_config
+        elif hasattr(app.state, 'embedding_config'):
+            delattr(app.state, 'embedding_config')
```

And fix the `EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError` constructor call in `test_search_dimension_mismatch_error` (~line 945):

```diff
-        mock_search.side_effect = EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError(
-            "Query dimension (384) != Index dimension (768)"
-        )
+        mock_search.side_effect = EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError(384, 768)
```

---

## Fix 7 — `backend/tests/test_database.py`: Pin exact `total_hits` assertion

```diff
         stats = database.get_cache_stats()
-        self.assertGreater(stats['total_hits'], 0)
+        self.assertEqual(stats['total_entries'], 1)
+        self.assertEqual(stats['total_hits'], 3)
```

And strengthen `test_cleanup_test_data` to verify actual row removal:

```diff
         counts = database.cleanup_test_data()

-        # Verify cleanup happened
-        self.assertIsInstance(counts, dict)
-        self.assertIn('files', counts)
-        self.assertIn('folders', counts)
-        self.assertIn('search_history', counts)
+        # Verify counts reflect what was removed
+        self.assertGreaterEqual(counts['files'], 1)
+        self.assertGreaterEqual(counts['folders'], 1)
+        self.assertGreaterEqual(counts['search_history'], 1)
+        # Verify rows are actually gone
+        self.assertIsNone(database.get_file_by_path('/test/path/file.pdf'))
+        folder_paths = [f['path'] for f in database.get_folder_history()]
+        self.assertNotIn('/test/folder', folder_paths)
+        history_queries = [h['query'] for h in database.get_search_history(limit=20)]
+        self.assertNotIn('test query', history_queries)
```

## Fix 8 — `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`: Add `get_embeddings` patch to `test_create_index_with_embedding_client`

```diff
+    `@patch`('backend.indexing.get_embeddings')
     `@patch`('backend.indexing.CharacterTextSplitter')
     `@patch`('backend.indexing.extract_text')
-    def test_create_index_with_embedding_client(self, mock_extract_text, mock_splitter_cls):
+    def test_create_index_with_embedding_client(self, mock_extract_text, mock_splitter_cls, mock_get_embeddings):
         ...
             self.assertIsNotNone(index)
             mock_embedding_client.embed_documents.assert_called()
+            mock_get_embeddings.assert_not_called()
```

And add executor patches to `TestIndexingProgressCallback.setUp` and `TestIndexingErrorRecovery.setUp` (the two classes that call `create_index()` without the synchronous executor patches):

```diff
 class TestIndexingProgressCallback(unittest.TestCase):
     def setUp(self):
         from backend import database
         database.init_database()
         self.temp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()
         for i in range(3):
             with open(os.path.join(self.temp_dir, f"file{i}.txt"), 'w') as f:
                 f.write(f"Content {i}")
+        self.pp_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor', side_effect=MockExecutor)
+        self.tp_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor', side_effect=MockExecutor)
+        self.ac_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.as_completed', side_effect=lambda fs: fs)
+        self.pp_patcher.start()
+        self.tp_patcher.start()
+        self.ac_patcher.start()

     def tearDown(self):
+        self.pp_patcher.stop()
+        self.tp_patcher.stop()
+        self.ac_patcher.stop()
         if os.path.exists(self.temp_dir):
             shutil.rmtree(self.temp_dir)
```

Apply the same pattern to `TestIndexingErrorRecovery.setUp`/`tearDown`.

---

-        // Should handle error gracefully (no crash)
-        await waitFor(() => expect(axios.get).toHaveBeenCalled())
+        // Should have attempted the browse call specifically
+        await waitFor(() => {
+            expect(
+                axios.get.mock.calls.some(([url]) => url.includes('/api/browse'))
+            ).toBe(true)
+        })
+        // Modal should still be visible (no crash / unmount)
+        expect(screen.getByText('Settings')).toBeDefined()
```

**Actionable comments posted: 4**

<details>
<summary>♻️ Duplicate comments (6)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>backend/tests/test_database.py (2)</summary><blockquote>

`504-506`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Assert the exact cache-hit total.**

`cache_response()` starts the row at `hit_count = 1`, and each successful `get_cached_response()` adds one more hit. After two reads on a fresh table, this should be `3`; `> 0` still passes if the read-side increment breaks.  
 

<details>
<summary>Suggested tightening</summary>

```diff
         # Verify hit count increased
         stats = database.get_cache_stats()
-        self.assertGreater(stats['total_hits'], 0)
+        self.assertEqual(stats['total_entries'], 1)
+        self.assertEqual(stats['total_hits'], 3)
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@backend/tests/test_database.py` around lines 504 - 506, The test currently
only asserts stats['total_hits'] > 0; change it to assert the exact expected
total of 3 since cache_response() seeds hit_count = 1 and two subsequent
get_cached_response() reads should increment it twice; update the assertion in
this test to self.assertEqual(database.get_cache_stats()['total_hits'], 3)
(referencing cache_response(), get_cached_response(), and
database.get_cache_stats()) so the test fails if hit counting stops
incrementing.
```

</details>

---

`613-620`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Verify that `cleanup_test_data()` actually removed the inserted rows.**

This only checks the returned dict shape, so a no-op cleanup can still pass. Assert that the file, folder, and search-history records inserted by this test are gone after cleanup, and that the returned counts reflect those deletions.  
 

<details>
<summary>Suggested assertions</summary>

```diff
         # Run cleanup
         counts = database.cleanup_test_data()
 
-        # Verify cleanup happened
-        self.assertIsInstance(counts, dict)
-        self.assertIn('files', counts)
-        self.assertIn('folders', counts)
-        self.assertIn('search_history', counts)
+        self.assertGreaterEqual(counts['files'], 1)
+        self.assertGreaterEqual(counts['folders'], 1)
+        self.assertGreaterEqual(counts['search_history'], 1)
+        self.assertIsNone(database.get_file_by_path('/test/path/file.pdf'))
+        self.assertNotIn('/test/folder', [item['path'] for item in database.get_folder_history()])
+        self.assertNotIn('test query', [item['query'] for item in database.get_search_history(limit=20)])
```
</details>

<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@backend/tests/test_database.py` around lines 613 - 620, The test currently
only checks the shape of the dict returned by cleanup_test_data(); after calling
database.cleanup_test_data() you must also assert that the specific test rows
were actually removed and that the returned counts match those deletions. Call
the appropriate lookup methods for the records you inserted (e.g.,
database.get_file_by_id(file_id), database.get_folder_by_id(folder_id),
database.get_search_history_by_id(search_id) or equivalent query helpers),
assert those return None/empty (or 0 rows), and assert counts['files'],
counts['folders'], counts['search_history'] equal the number of rows removed for
each type returned by cleanup_test_data().

</blockquote></details>
<details>
<summary>backend/tests/test_api.py (2)</summary><blockquote>

`791-793`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Restore `app.state.embedding_config` around this class.**

These tests share the singleton `app`, and the real embeddings handler caches config on `app.state.embedding_config`. Without snapshot/restore, one test can change the next test’s starting state.  
  

<details>
<summary>Suggested isolation</summary>

```diff
 class TestAPIEmbeddingSettings(unittest.TestCase):
     """Test cases for embedding settings endpoints."""
 
     def setUp(self):
         """Set up test client before each test method."""
+        self._had_embedding_config = hasattr(app.state, 'embedding_config')
+        self._original_embedding_config = getattr(app.state, 'embedding_config', None)
         self.client = TestClient(app)
+
+    def tearDown(self):
+        self.client.close()
+        if self._had_embedding_config:
+            app.state.embedding_config = self._original_embedding_config
+        elif hasattr(app.state, 'embedding_config'):
+            delattr(app.state, 'embedding_config')
```
</details>

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "Inspect cached embedding-config usage and test cleanup:"
rg -n -C2 'embedding_config|class TestAPIEmbeddingSettings|def setUp|def tearDown' \
  backend/settings.py backend/tests/test_api.py backend/api.py
```

Based on learnings: Cache embedding configuration in `app.state.embedding_config` and use `get_active_embedding_client(app)` helper for resolving providers.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@backend/tests/test_api.py` around lines 791 - 793, Tests share the global
FastAPI app and its cached embedding config, so modify the TestAPI
setUp/tearDown to snapshot and restore app.state.embedding_config around each
test: in setUp (where TestClient(app) is created) save the current
app.state.embedding_config to a temp variable and in tearDown restore it (or
delete if it was absent) so tests don't leak config; also update tests to
resolve embedding clients via the existing get_active_embedding_client(app)
helper and ensure any code paths that mutate app.state.embedding_config (or the
TestClient usage) are cleaned up between tests.
```

</details>

---

`949-952`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Construct `EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError` with dimensions, not a message string.**

This exception takes `query_dim` and `index_dim`. Passing a single string raises `TypeError` during test setup, so the test never reaches the `409` path it is supposed to cover.  
 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
         mock_config.return_value.get.return_value = 'openai'
         mock_search.side_effect = EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError(
-            "Query dimension (384) != Index dimension (768)"
+            384, 768
         )
```
</details>

Based on learnings: Search functionality must handle embedding dimension mismatches and return 409 Conflict if FAISS index dimension differs from query vector.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@backend/tests/test_api.py` around lines 949 - 952, The test's
mock_search.side_effect is constructing EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError
incorrectly with a message string; change it to instantiate the exception with
the expected dimensions (e.g., EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError(384, 768) or
named args query_dim=384, index_dim=768) so the test triggers the
embedding-dimension mismatch path in the code that should return a 409 Conflict;
update the instantiation referenced by mock_search.side_effect and keep the
surrounding mocking of mock_config.return_value.get intact.
```

</details>

</blockquote></details>

<summary>backend/tests/test_indexing.py (2)</summary><blockquote>

`960-989`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Patch `backend.indexing.get_embeddings` here and assert it stays unused.**

If `create_index()` regresses and ignores `embedding_client`, this test can still fall through the real fallback path and won’t actually prove the injected-client contract. Patch `get_embeddings` in this test and add `assert_not_called()`.

<details>
<summary>🛡️ Suggested hardening</summary>

```diff
     `@patch`('backend.indexing.CharacterTextSplitter')
     `@patch`('backend.indexing.extract_text')
-    def test_create_index_with_embedding_client(self, mock_extract_text, mock_splitter_cls):
+    `@patch`('backend.indexing.get_embeddings')
+    def test_create_index_with_embedding_client(
+        self,
+        mock_get_embeddings,
+        mock_extract_text,
+        mock_splitter_cls,
+    ):
         """Test create_index with embedding_client parameter."""
@@
             self.assertIsNotNone(index)
             # Verify embedding client was used (not get_embeddings)
             mock_embedding_client.embed_documents.assert_called()
+            mock_get_embeddings.assert_not_called()
```
</details>

As per coding guidelines, "Mock LLM and network calls in backend tests".

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@backend/tests/test_indexing.py` around lines 960 - 989, Patch
backend.indexing.get_embeddings in this test and assert it is not called to
ensure create_index uses the injected embedding_client; specifically, add a
patch for backend.indexing.get_embeddings (or use `@patch` on get_embeddings) in
the test_create_index_with_embedding_client setup, then after calling
create_index(self.temp_dir, provider="openai", api_key="fake_key",
embedding_client=mock_embedding_client) assert
get_embeddings.assert_not_called() and keep the existing assert that
mock_embedding_client.embed_documents.assert_called() so the test fails if
create_index ignores the embedding_client and falls back to get_embeddings.
```

</details>

---

`1149-1171`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Reuse the synchronous executor patches in these remaining `create_index()` test classes.**

Unlike the earlier indexing tests, these classes still let `create_index()` construct real executors. That can spawn worker threads/processes, bypass local mocks, and turn the tests into flaky CI-only failures. Mirror the `MockExecutor` patch/start/stop block already used elsewhere in this file.

<details>
<summary>🧵 Reuse the existing synchronous executor patch block</summary>

```diff
     def setUp(self):
         """
         Prepare test environment by initializing the test database and creating a temporary directory with three sample text files.
         """
         from backend import database
         database.init_database()
         self.temp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()

         # Create multiple test files
         for i in range(3):
             with open(os.path.join(self.temp_dir, f"file{i}.txt"), 'w') as f:
                 f.write(f"Content {i}")
+
+        self.pp_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor', side_effect=MockExecutor)
+        self.tp_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor', side_effect=MockExecutor)
+        self.ac_patcher = patch('concurrent.futures.as_completed', side_effect=lambda fs: fs)
+        self.pp_patcher.start()
+        self.tp_patcher.start()
+        self.ac_patcher.start()

     def tearDown(self):
         """
         Remove the per-test temporary directory created in setUp.
         """
+        self.pp_patcher.stop()
+        self.tp_patcher.stop()
+        self.ac_patcher.stop()
         if os.path.exists(self.temp_dir):
             shutil.rmtree(self.temp_dir)
```

Apply the same change to `TestIndexingErrorRecovery`.
</details>

 


Also applies to: 1233-1248

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@backend/tests/test_indexing.py` around lines 1149 - 1171, Tests under
TestIndexingErrorRecovery (and similar classes) call create_index() which
currently constructs real executors and can spawn worker threads; update these
test classes to reuse the existing synchronous executor patch block
(MockExecutor) used elsewhere in this file by adding the same patch/start in
setUp and stop/cleanup in tearDown so create_index() uses the mocked executor;
specifically, in the TestIndexingErrorRecovery class add the MockExecutor patch
initialization in setUp (mirroring the existing patch block used in other
indexing tests) and ensure the corresponding patch.stop() (or cleanup) is called
in tearDown to avoid real worker threads being created.
```

</details>

</blockquote></details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@backend/tests/test_api.py`:
- Around line 839-847: The test_update_embedding_settings_missing_api_key test
currently allows either 400 or 422 which weakens the contract; update the
assertion to expect the explicit 422 validation response from POST
/api/settings/embeddings when provider_type is "commercial_api" and api_key is
missing. Modify the assertion in test_update_embedding_settings_missing_api_key
to assert response.status_code == 422 (and optionally assert the validation
error key/message in the response body) so the test tightly reflects the app's
validation behavior for embedding settings.

In `@backend/tests/test_indexing.py`:
- Around line 1257-1262: The test currently catches any Exception from
create_index, so make the assertion targeted to the mocked embedding failure by
expecting the specific error text or type from mock_get_embeddings: change the
side_effect to raise a distinct error (e.g., Exception("Embedding API error"))
and replace with self.assertRaisesRegex(Exception, r"Embedding API error"):
create_index(self.temp_dir, "openai", "fake_key") so only the embedding path
triggers the test; alternatively define and raise a custom EmbeddingError in the
mock and assertRaises(EmbeddingError) to reference the embedding failure
directly.
- Around line 1223-1227: The test currently only checks that more than two
stages appear, which is weak; update the assertion in
backend/tests/test_indexing.py to validate the full expected stage set emitted
by backend/indexing.py by comparing the discovered stages (found_stages) against
the canonical stage_keywords list (or asserting found_stages contains all
stage_keywords) so the test fails if any of the seven labels (Extracting,
Chunking, Embedding, Keyword, Clustering, Summarizing, Finalizing) are missing
or renamed; locate the variables messages, stage_keywords, and found_stages in
the test and replace the self.assertGreater(...) with an assertion that checks
set(found_stages) >= set(stage_keywords) (or equality) to enforce the full
progress stage contract.

In `@frontend/src/test/SettingsModal.test.jsx`:
- Around line 469-489: The test "dismisses toast after 3 seconds" uses
vi.useFakeTimers() but advances timers without flushing React updates; wrap the
timer advance in React's act so state updates are applied before assertions.
Specifically, in SettingsModal.test.jsx around the vi.advanceTimersByTime(3000)
call, import and use act (from react-dom/test-utils or `@testing-library/react`)
and replace the standalone vi.advanceTimersByTime(3000) with act(() => {
vi.advanceTimersByTime(3000) }); keep the surrounding waitFor/assertions and the
final vi.useRealTimers() as-is.

---

Duplicate comments:
In `@backend/tests/test_api.py`:
- Around line 791-793: Tests share the global FastAPI app and its cached
embedding config, so modify the TestAPI setUp/tearDown to snapshot and restore
app.state.embedding_config around each test: in setUp (where TestClient(app) is
created) save the current app.state.embedding_config to a temp variable and in
tearDown restore it (or delete if it was absent) so tests don't leak config;
also update tests to resolve embedding clients via the existing
get_active_embedding_client(app) helper and ensure any code paths that mutate
app.state.embedding_config (or the TestClient usage) are cleaned up between
tests.

EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError incorrectly with a message string; change it to
instantiate the exception with the expected dimensions (e.g.,
EmbeddingDimensionMismatchError(384, 768) or named args query_dim=384,
index_dim=768) so the test triggers the embedding-dimension mismatch path in the
code that should return a 409 Conflict; update the instantiation referenced by
mock_search.side_effect and keep the surrounding mocking of
mock_config.return_value.get intact.

In `@backend/tests/test_database.py`:
- Around line 504-506: The test currently only asserts stats['total_hits'] > 0;
change it to assert the exact expected total of 3 since cache_response() seeds
hit_count = 1 and two subsequent get_cached_response() reads should increment it
twice; update the assertion in this test to
self.assertEqual(database.get_cache_stats()['total_hits'], 3) (referencing
cache_response(), get_cached_response(), and database.get_cache_stats()) so the
test fails if hit counting stops incrementing.
- Around line 613-620: The test currently only checks the shape of the dict
returned by cleanup_test_data(); after calling database.cleanup_test_data() you
must also assert that the specific test rows were actually removed and that the
returned counts match those deletions. Call the appropriate lookup methods for
the records you inserted (e.g., database.get_file_by_id(file_id),
database.get_folder_by_id(folder_id),
database.get_search_history_by_id(search_id) or equivalent query helpers),
assert those return None/empty (or 0 rows), and assert counts['files'],
counts['folders'], counts['search_history'] equal the number of rows removed for
each type returned by cleanup_test_data().

In `@backend/tests/test_indexing.py`:
- Around line 960-989: Patch backend.indexing.get_embeddings in this test and
assert it is not called to ensure create_index uses the injected
embedding_client; specifically, add a patch for backend.indexing.get_embeddings
(or use `@patch` on get_embeddings) in the test_create_index_with_embedding_client
setup, then after calling create_index(self.temp_dir, provider="openai",
api_key="fake_key", embedding_client=mock_embedding_client) assert
get_embeddings.assert_not_called() and keep the existing assert that
mock_embedding_client.embed_documents.assert_called() so the test fails if
create_index ignores the embedding_client and falls back to get_embeddings.
- Around line 1149-1171: Tests under TestIndexingErrorRecovery (and similar
classes) call create_index() which currently constructs real executors and can
spawn worker threads; update these test classes to reuse the existing
synchronous executor patch block (MockExecutor) used elsewhere in this file by
adding the same patch/start in setUp and stop/cleanup in tearDown so
create_index() uses the mocked executor; specifically, in the
TestIndexingErrorRecovery class add the MockExecutor patch initialization in
setUp (mirroring the existing patch block used in other indexing tests) and
ensure the corresponding patch.stop() (or cleanup) is called in tearDown to
avoid real worker threads being created.
```

</details>

---

<details>
<summary>🧰 Additional context used</summary>

<details>
<summary>📓 Path-based instructions (9)</summary>

<details>
<summary>backend/**/*.py</summary>


**📄 CodeRabbit inference engine (.github/copilot-instructions.md)**

> `backend/**/*.py`: Backend code should be placed in `backend/` directory including `api.py`, `database.py`, `indexing.py`, `search.py`, `file_processing.py`, `llm_integration.py`, `model_manager.py`, `background.py`
> Python code should target Python 3.8+ compatibility while CI uses Python 3.10
> 
> `backend/**/*.py`: Redact sensitive search queries and PII in logs to prevent data leakage
> Implement lazy loading for heavy backend modules (langchain, llama-cpp, tkinter) to reduce cold-start time

Files:
- `backend/tests/test_database.py`
- `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`
- `backend/tests/test_api.py`

</details>
<details>
<summary>backend/tests/**/*.py</summary>


**📄 CodeRabbit inference engine (.github/copilot-instructions.md)**

> `backend/tests/**/*.py`: Backend tests should be placed in `backend/tests/` directory
> Mock LLM and network calls in backend tests
> Tests that manipulate DB should auto-use SQLite in `data/metadata.db` and clean via `database.cleanup_test_data()` on success
> 
> `backend/tests/**/*.py`: Backend tests MUST be placed in `backend/tests/` and follow pytest conventions
> Always mock LLM calls in tests to avoid API costs using `@patch` decorator

Files:
- `backend/tests/test_database.py`
- `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`
- `backend/tests/test_api.py`

</details>
<details>
<summary>**/*.py</summary>


**📄 CodeRabbit inference engine (AGENTS.md)**

> `**/*.py`: Backend code MUST be placed in `backend/` or `backend/tests/` directories. Exception: `api.py` is the entry point (in `backend/`). NO `.py` files in the project root
> All generated files (`.db`, `.faiss`, `.log`, `.json` results) MUST go in `data/` directory and code must reference `DATA_DIR` constant

Files:
- `backend/tests/test_database.py`
- `backend/tests/test_indexing.py`
- `backend/tests/test_api.py`

</details>
<details>
<summary>frontend/src/**/*.{jsx,js,ts,tsx}</summary>


**📄 CodeRabbit inference engine (.github/copilot-instructions.md)**

> Frontend code should be placed in `frontend/src/` including `App.jsx` and components/

Files:
- `frontend/src/test/SettingsModal.test.jsx`

</details>

- `frontend/src/test/SettingsModal.test.jsx`

<details>
<summary>🔇 Additional comments (6)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>frontend/src/test/SettingsModal.test.jsx (5)</summary><blockquote>

`399-418`: **The browse-error assertion remains vacuous.**

The assertion `expect(axios.get).toHaveBeenCalled()` is satisfied by the initial settings fetches (`/api/config`, `/api/index/status`, etc.) before the "Add Folder" click. This test never proves the `/api/browse` failure path executed.

Assert the specific browse request and verify a visible post-click outcome:

<details>
<summary>✅ Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
         // Click add folder (should not crash)
         fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Add Folder'))

-        // Should handle error gracefully (no crash)
-        await waitFor(() => expect(axios.get).toHaveBeenCalled())
+        // Verify browse endpoint was called
+        await waitFor(() => {
+            expect(
+                axios.get.mock.calls.some(([url]) => url.includes('/api/browse'))
+            ).toBe(true)
+        })
+        // Modal should still be visible (no crash)
+        expect(screen.getByText('Settings')).toBeDefined()
```
</details>

Based on learnings, "Frontend should use `testing-library/react` interactions instead of basic logic checks in tests for better coverage".

---

`95-102`: **LGTM!**

The test correctly navigates to the "Data Management" section before asserting the "Clear Search History" behavior, aligning with the new two-pane layout design.

---

`212-241`: **LGTM!**

Good coverage for section navigation. Tests verify that clicking each sidebar section renders the expected content (folders, AI Provider options, ModelManager mock, Data Management).

---

`243-281`: **LGTM!**

Solid coverage for indexing status states—both in-progress with percentage/current file, and error scenarios. The mock overrides are structured clearly.

---

`365-397`: **LGTM!**

This test validates the important security behavior of not sending placeholder API keys (`'••••••••'`) to the server. The assertion correctly checks that `api_key` is undefined in the POST payload when the user hasn't modified the masked value.

</blockquote></details>
<details>
<summary>backend/tests/test_indexing.py (1)</summary><blockquote>

`1044-1081`: **Nice coverage on the metadata sidecar contract.**

These tests lock down both halves of the new `_meta.json` behavior: `save_index()` writes `model_name`/`embedding_dim`, and `load_index()` returns them as the 8th tuple element. Based on learnings, "Persist embedding metadata in `_meta.json` file containing `model_name` and `embedding_dim` alongside `.faiss` index".



Also applies to: 1104-1143

</blockquote></details>

</blockquote></details>

</details>
